{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Account Manager (Office Buildings)",
  "description": "Arnergy is a distributed utility providing energy reliability to SMEs and economic clusters in emerging market with flexible acquisition options including energy Subscription (ES) based on monthly fixed fee, consumer financed Lease to Own (LO) with monthly instalment or a Outright Sales (OS) option. Arnergy is on track to deploy excess of 100 Megawatt installed PV capacity powering more than 35,000 businesses across impact sectors not limited to Healthcare, Education, Hospitality by 2023. If you are excited to be part of the solution to Africa&#39;s energy crisis starting with Nigeria, we are happy to have you on board.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Account Manager (Office Buildings)

Job Location: Lagos

Description


	As Account Manager, Office Buildings, you will drive Arnergy&#39;s commercial sales into the corporate real estate and office occupier segment.
	You will position Arnergy&#39;s solar PV, battery storage, and energy management systems as the premium solution for facility managers, property developers, and corporate occupiers.


You will:
Vertical-Specific Sales &amp; Business Development


	Identify, target, and develop new accounts across commercial office towers, corporate headquarters, business parks, co-working hubs, and mixed-use developments.
	Map energy consumption, generator capacities, diesel spend, and ESG reporting obligations within target accounts to build a data-driven value proposition.
	Target property developers and real estate investment companies to embed Arnergy as the preferred solar partner in new developments at design stage.
	Engage corporate occupiers directly, particularly professional services firms, banks, telecoms companies, and multinationals with energy cost and ESG reporting mandates.


Solution Scoping &amp; Proposal Development:


	Work with Technical Services to scope systems for large commercial buildings: rooftop PV, carport installations, and battery storage for grid/generator backup.
	Develop financial models covering TCO, diesel displacement, capital expenditure vs. financing options, and payback period &mdash; tailored to corporate financial decision frameworks.
	Prepare and present Board-level proposals and investment papers, adapting technical content for executive and non-technical audiences.
	Develop multi-building proposals for property groups or facility management companies managing several office sites.


Account Management &amp; Relationship Development:


	Manage and grow a portfolio of active office building accounts, maintaining high satisfaction, visibility into system performance, and readiness to expand to additional floors, buildings, or corporate campuses.
	Proactively engage facility managers and estate companies around planned building upgrades, expansions, or new tenant requirements that could trigger additional energy system needs.
	Track energy performance data for key accounts and present periodic energy savings reports to reinforce value and identify upsell opportunities.
	Serve as the primary commercial contact for account escalations, coordinating swiftly with CX, O&amp;M, and Technical teams.


Reporting &amp; Market Intelligence:


	Submit accurate daily, weekly, and monthly pipeline and revenue reports via Arnergy&#39;s CRM.
	Monitor commercial real estate developments &mdash; new office tower completions, building
	refurbishments, corporate relocations &mdash; and convert intelligence into proactive pipeline activity.
	Report on competitor activity, market pricing trends, and customer objections in the corporate real estate space.


Requirements and Skills
You&#39;ll be a good fit for this role if you have the experience below:


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Business Administration, Marketing, Science, Engineering or a related field.
	Minimum 4 years of B2B sales experience, with at least 2 years selling to commercial real estate, facility management, or corporate accounts in Nigeria.
	Demonstrable track record of winning and managing high-value corporate accounts requiring multi-stakeholder engagement.
	Strong commercial and financial fluency &mdash; able to construct and defend investment cases, ROI models, and payback analyses at board level.
	Excellent written and verbal communication &mdash; proposal writing, formal presentations, and board-level engagement.
	Proficiency in CRM tools and sales reporting platforms.


We would also like to see:


	Experience with large-ticket capital equipment, property technology, or energy solution sales in a corporate real estate context.
	Understanding of solar PV, battery storage, or energy management systems (full technical training will be provided).
	Experience with asset financing sales models.
	A genuine hunter mentality &mdash; self-driven, resilient, and motivated by closing deals.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-06-16",
  "validThrough": "2026-07-17",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Arnergy Solar Limited"
    
  },

    "@type": "Place",
    "address": {
      "@type": "PostalAddress",
       "postalCode": ". ",
       "addressRegion": ". ",
       "streetAddress": ". ",
      "addressLocality": "Lagos",
      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }
  },

    "value": "200491"
  },

    "@type": "MonetaryAmount",
    "currency": "NGN",

      "minValue": 0,
      "maxValue": 0,
      "unitText": "MONTH"
    }

}



Account Manager (Office Buildings) Job at Arnergy Solar Limited




        
    

        
        Arnergy is a distributed utility providing energy reliability to SMEs and economic clusters in emerging market with flexible acquisition options including energy Subscription (ES) based on monthly fixed fee, consumer financed Lease to Own (LO) with monthly instalment or a Outright Sales (OS) option. Arnergy is on track to deploy excess of 100 Megawatt installed PV capacity powering more than 35,000 businesses across impact sectors not limited to Healthcare, Education, Hospitality by 2023. If you are excited to be part of the solution to Africa's energy crisis starting with Nigeria, we are happy to have you on board.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Account Manager (Office Buildings)
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Lagos

Description


	As Account Manager, Office Buildings, you will drive Arnergy's commercial sales into the corporate real estate and office occupier segment.
	You will position Arnergy's solar PV, battery storage, and energy management systems as the premium solution for facility managers, property developers, and corporate occupiers.


You will:
Vertical-Specific Sales & Business Development


	Identify, target, and develop new accounts across commercial office towers, corporate headquarters, business parks, co-working hubs, and mixed-use developments.
	Map energy consumption, generator capacities, diesel spend, and ESG reporting obligations within target accounts to build a data-driven value proposition.
	Target property developers and real estate investment companies to embed Arnergy as the preferred solar partner in new developments at design stage.
	Engage corporate occupiers directly, particularly professional services firms, banks, telecoms companies, and multinationals with energy cost and ESG reporting mandates.


Solution Scoping & Proposal Development:


	Work with Technical Services to scope systems for large commercial buildings: rooftop PV, carport installations, and battery storage for grid/generator backup.
	Develop financial models covering TCO, diesel displacement, capital expenditure vs. financing options, and payback period — tailored to corporate financial decision frameworks.
	Prepare and present Board-level proposals and investment papers, adapting technical content for executive and non-technical audiences.
	Develop multi-building proposals for property groups or facility management companies managing several office sites.


Account Management & Relationship Development:


	Manage and grow a portfolio of active office building accounts, maintaining high satisfaction, visibility into system performance, and readiness to expand to additional floors, buildings, or corporate campuses.
	Proactively engage facility managers and estate companies around planned building upgrades, expansions, or new tenant requirements that could trigger additional energy system needs.
	Track energy performance data for key accounts and present periodic energy savings reports to reinforce value and identify upsell opportunities.
	Serve as the primary commercial contact for account escalations, coordinating swiftly with CX, O&M, and Technical teams.


Reporting & Market Intelligence:


	Submit accurate daily, weekly, and monthly pipeline and revenue reports via Arnergy's CRM.
	Monitor commercial real estate developments — new office tower completions, building
	refurbishments, corporate relocations — and convert intelligence into proactive pipeline activity.
	Report on competitor activity, market pricing trends, and customer objections in the corporate real estate space.


Requirements and Skills
You'll be a good fit for this role if you have the experience below:


	Bachelor's Degree in Business Administration, Marketing, Science, Engineering or a related field.
	Minimum 4 years of B2B sales experience, with at least 2 years selling to commercial real estate, facility management, or corporate accounts in Nigeria.
	Demonstrable track record of winning and managing high-value corporate accounts requiring multi-stakeholder engagement.
	Strong commercial and financial fluency — able to construct and defend investment cases, ROI models, and payback analyses at board level.
	Excellent written and verbal communication — proposal writing, formal presentations, and board-level engagement.
	Proficiency in CRM tools and sales reporting platforms.


We would also like to see:


	Experience with large-ticket capital equipment, property technology, or energy solution sales in a corporate real estate context.
	Understanding of solar PV, battery storage, or energy management systems (full technical training will be provided).
	Experience with asset financing sales models.
	A genuine hunter mentality — self-driven, resilient, and motivated by closing deals.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Digital Marketing Manager",
  "description": "ICS Outsourcing is Nigeria&rsquo;s leading Outsourcing provider incorporated in August 1994. We offer a comprehensive range of outsourcing services ranging from People Outsourcing to Sales BPO. We provide bespoke Business Support Solutions to all kinds and sizes of business.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Digital Marketing Manager

Job Location: Lagos Mainland, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Industry: Agrochemical

Role Summary


	To build and lead the company&rsquo;s digital presence from the ground up, driving brand awareness, lead generation, and revenue growth across Nigeria&rsquo;s agricultural ecosystem.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and implement a full-scale digital marketing strategy aligned with business goals
	Manage website, social media platforms, and online brand positioning
	Plan and execute paid campaigns (Google Ads, Meta Ads, etc.)
	Drive lead generation campaigns targeting distributors, agro-dealers, and farmers
	Coordinate with branch teams for localized campaigns
	Develop seasonal campaigns aligned with planting cycles
	Oversee content strategy (product education, farmer awareness, promotions)
	Monitor analytics (traffic, conversions, ROI) and optimize campaigns
	Manage marketing budget and ensure cost efficiency
	Explore digital channels relevant to rural/agricultural markets.


KPIs


	Lead generation &amp; conversion rates
	Website and social growth
	Campaign ROI
	Brand visibility.


Requirements


	Interested candidates should possess an HND / Bachelor`s / Master`s Degree
	5+ years of digital marketing experience
	Strong expertise in SEO, SEM, social media, and analytics tools
	Experience in FMCG, agriculture, or distribution preferred
	Industry experience in agrochemicals, fertilizers, Agro commodities, or distribution is highly preferred.
",

  "validThrough": "2026-07-01",

Digital Marketing Manager Job at ICS Outsourcing




        
    

        
        ICS Outsourcing is Nigeria’s leading Outsourcing provider incorporated in August 1994. We offer a comprehensive range of outsourcing services ranging from People Outsourcing to Sales BPO. We provide bespoke Business Support Solutions to all kinds and sizes of business.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Digital Marketing Manager

Job Location: Lagos Mainland, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Industry: Agrochemical

Role Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	To build and lead the company’s digital presence from the ground up, driving brand awareness, lead generation, and revenue growth across Nigeria’s agricultural ecosystem.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and implement a full-scale digital marketing strategy aligned with business goals
	Manage website, social media platforms, and online brand positioning
	Plan and execute paid campaigns (Google Ads, Meta Ads, etc.)
	Drive lead generation campaigns targeting distributors, agro-dealers, and farmers
	Coordinate with branch teams for localized campaigns
	Develop seasonal campaigns aligned with planting cycles
	Oversee content strategy (product education, farmer awareness, promotions)
	Monitor analytics (traffic, conversions, ROI) and optimize campaigns
	Manage marketing budget and ensure cost efficiency
	Explore digital channels relevant to rural/agricultural markets.


KPIs


	Lead generation & conversion rates
	Website and social growth
	Campaign ROI
	Brand visibility.


Requirements


	Interested candidates should possess an HND / Bachelor`s / Master`s Degree
	5+ years of digital marketing experience
	Strong expertise in SEO, SEM, social media, and analytics tools
	Experience in FMCG, agriculture, or distribution preferred
	Industry experience in agrochemicals, fertilizers, Agro commodities, or distribution is highly preferred.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Customer Sales Representative Job at Savalor HR Consulting

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Customer Sales Representative",
  "description": "Savalor HR Consulting - Our client, a reputable and fast-growing pharmaceutical company, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Sales Representative
Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking customer-focused, tech-savvy, and results-driven professionals who are passionate about delivering exceptional customer experiences while driving sales growth and building lasting customer relationships.


Key Responsibilities


	Respond to customer inquiries through phone, email, and digital channels.
	Process orders accurately and provide timely follow-up.
	Build and maintain strong customer relationships.
	Identify sales opportunities and recommend suitable products or solutions.
	Resolve customer complaints efficiently while ensuring high customer satisfaction.
	Maintain accurate customer records using CRM and digital tools.
	Collaborate with internal teams to deliver excellent customer service and achieve targets.


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree or HND in Business Administration, Marketing, Mass Communication, Economics, Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field.
	Minimum of 3 years of experience in Customer Service, Customer Sales, Sales Support, or a related role.
	Experience using CRM systems and digital customer engagement tools.
	Excellent communication and relationship management skills.
	Strong problem-solving, organizational, and multitasking abilities.
	Tech-savvy with proficiency in Microsoft Office and digital tools.


What We Are Looking For:


	We are looking for professionals who are customer-centric, proactive, digitally savvy, and passionate about delivering excellent service while achieving results.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-06-16",
  "validThrough": "2026-06-24",

    "@type": "Organization",

Customer Sales Representative Job at Savalor HR Consulting




        
    

        
        Savalor HR Consulting - Our client, a reputable and fast-growing pharmaceutical company, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Sales Representative
Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking customer-focused, tech-savvy, and results-driven professionals who are passionate about delivering exceptional customer experiences while driving sales growth and building lasting customer relationships.


Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Respond to customer inquiries through phone, email, and digital channels.
	Process orders accurately and provide timely follow-up.
	Build and maintain strong customer relationships.
	Identify sales opportunities and recommend suitable products or solutions.
	Resolve customer complaints efficiently while ensuring high customer satisfaction.
	Maintain accurate customer records using CRM and digital tools.
	Collaborate with internal teams to deliver excellent customer service and achieve targets.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree or HND in Business Administration, Marketing, Mass Communication, Economics, Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field.
	Minimum of 3 years of experience in Customer Service, Customer Sales, Sales Support, or a related role.
	Experience using CRM systems and digital customer engagement tools.
	Excellent communication and relationship management skills.
	Strong problem-solving, organizational, and multitasking abilities.
	Tech-savvy with proficiency in Microsoft Office and digital tools.


What We Are Looking For:


	We are looking for professionals who are customer-centric, proactive, digitally savvy, and passionate about delivering excellent service while achieving results.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Data Center Officer",
  "description": "Wema Bank Plc - Widely reputed as the longest surviving and most resilient indigenous bank in Nigeria, Wema Bank Plc has over the years, diligently offered a fully-fledged range of value-adding banking and financial advisory services to the Nigerian public.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Data Center Officer

Job Location: Lagos

Job Summary


	The Data Center Officer is responsible for the day-to-day operations, maintenance, and monitoring of Wema Bank&#39;s data center facilities and infrastructure.
	The role ensures the continuous availability, performance, and security of data center resources&mdash;including servers, storage, networking equipment, and environmental controls&mdash;in support of the bank&#39;s critical IT services.


Responsibilities


	Monitor and maintain data center infrastructure including servers, storage systems, network equipment, UPS, cooling, and power systems.
	Ensure the availability and reliability of all data center resources by performing routine health checks and proactive maintenance.
	Respond to and resolve data center incidents and alerts, minimizing downtime and service disruptions.
	Manage and document data center assets, including hardware inventory, rack layouts, cable management, and equipment lifecycle.
	Support the implementation of disaster recovery and business continuity plans, including DR drills and failover testing.
	Ensure physical security of the data center, including access control management, CCTV monitoring, and visitor logging.
	Coordinate with vendors and third-party providers for equipment maintenance, warranty services, and hardware replacements.
	Implement and adhere to data center standards, best practices, and change management procedures.
	Perform capacity planning and make recommendations for infrastructure upgrades to support business growth.
	Maintain accurate and up-to-date documentation of data center configurations, procedures, and incident logs.
	Support cloud infrastructure integration and hybrid cloud connectivity with on-premises data center resources.
	Ensure compliance with environmental, safety, and regulatory requirements applicable to data center operations.


Requirements
Education:


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Computer Science, Electrical/Electronic Engineering, Information Technology, or related field.





Professional Certification:


	Data Center Certified Associate (DCCA), CompTIA Server+, or equivalent certification.
	VMware VCP or CCNA is an added advantage.


Experience:


	3&ndash;5 years of experience in data center operations or IT infrastructure management.
	Experience in a financial services or enterprise environment is preferred.


Specialized Knowledge:


	Knowledge of data center operations, including server hardware (HP, Dell, IBM), SAN/NAS storage, network infrastructure, and environmental management systems.
	Understanding of virtualization technologies (VMware, Hyper-V) and cloud platforms.


Skills:


	Strong technical troubleshooting and problem-solving skills.
	Ability to work effectively under pressure and in a 24/7 operational environment.
	Good documentation and communication skills.
	Attention to detail and adherence to operational procedures.


Benefits
At Wema Bank, we recognize and reward the contributions of our employees by offering a range of benefits designed to enhance their well-being and work-life balance. Here are some key perks you can enjoy as a Knight.&nbsp;


	Healthcare &ndash; We have a comprehensive medical coverage to ensure you, and your loved ones stay healthy.
	Elderly Care - At Wema Bank, our robust HMO plans include a provision for elderly care, allowing you to enroll one of your parents under your health coverage.
	Parental Leave (Paternity &amp; Maternity) &ndash; Paid leave to support new parents, allowing them time to bond with their newborns.
	Year-End Bonus (13th Month) &ndash; As a Knight, there is an opportunity for you to enjoy an additional allowance as a reward for your dedication and hard work.
	Company Events &ndash; At Wema Bank, we engage in team-building activities, networking opportunities, and celebrations that foster a strong workplace culture.
	Competitive Pay &ndash; Attractive salary packages that reflect your skills, experience, and contributions.
	Leave Allowance &ndash; Financial support when you take your well-deserved time off.
	Profit Sharing &ndash; A share in the bank&rsquo;s success, ensuring you, as an employee, benefit from the company&rsquo;s growth.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-06-16",
  "validThrough": "2026-06-27",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Wema Bank Plc"
    
  },
  "jobLocation": {
    "@type": "Place",
    "address": {
      "@type": "PostalAddress",
       "postalCode": ". ",
       "addressRegion": ". ",
       "streetAddress": ". ",
      "addressLocality": "Lagos",
      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }
  },
  "identifier": {
    "@type": "PropertyValue",
    "name": "",
    "value": "200488"
  },
  "baseSalary": {
    "@type": "MonetaryAmount",
    "currency": "NGN",
    "value": {
      "@type": "QuantitativeValue",
      "minValue": 0,
      "maxValue": 0,
      "unitText": "MONTH"
    }
  }
}



Data Center Officer Job at Wema Bank Plc




        
    

        
        Wema Bank Plc - Widely reputed as the longest surviving and most resilient indigenous bank in Nigeria, Wema Bank Plc has over the years, diligently offered a fully-fledged range of value-adding banking and financial advisory services to the Nigerian public.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Data Center Officer

Job Location: Lagos

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Data Center Officer is responsible for the day-to-day operations, maintenance, and monitoring of Wema Bank's data center facilities and infrastructure.
	The role ensures the continuous availability, performance, and security of data center resources—including servers, storage, networking equipment, and environmental controls—in support of the bank's critical IT services.


Responsibilities


	Monitor and maintain data center infrastructure including servers, storage systems, network equipment, UPS, cooling, and power systems.
	Ensure the availability and reliability of all data center resources by performing routine health checks and proactive maintenance.
	Respond to and resolve data center incidents and alerts, minimizing downtime and service disruptions.
	Manage and document data center assets, including hardware inventory, rack layouts, cable management, and equipment lifecycle.
	Support the implementation of disaster recovery and business continuity plans, including DR drills and failover testing.
	Ensure physical security of the data center, including access control management, CCTV monitoring, and visitor logging.
	Coordinate with vendors and third-party providers for equipment maintenance, warranty services, and hardware replacements.
	Implement and adhere to data center standards, best practices, and change management procedures.
	Perform capacity planning and make recommendations for infrastructure upgrades to support business growth.
	Maintain accurate and up-to-date documentation of data center configurations, procedures, and incident logs.
	Support cloud infrastructure integration and hybrid cloud connectivity with on-premises data center resources.
	Ensure compliance with environmental, safety, and regulatory requirements applicable to data center operations.


Requirements
Education:


	Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science, Electrical/Electronic Engineering, Information Technology, or related field.





Professional Certification:


	Data Center Certified Associate (DCCA), CompTIA Server+, or equivalent certification.
	VMware VCP or CCNA is an added advantage.


Experience:


	3–5 years of experience in data center operations or IT infrastructure management.
	Experience in a financial services or enterprise environment is preferred.


Specialized Knowledge:


	Knowledge of data center operations, including server hardware (HP, Dell, IBM), SAN/NAS storage, network infrastructure, and environmental management systems.
	Understanding of virtualization technologies (VMware, Hyper-V) and cloud platforms.


Skills:


	Strong technical troubleshooting and problem-solving skills.
	Ability to work effectively under pressure and in a 24/7 operational environment.
	Good documentation and communication skills.
	Attention to detail and adherence to operational procedures.


Benefits
At Wema Bank, we recognize and reward the contributions of our employees by offering a range of benefits designed to enhance their well-being and work-life balance. Here are some key perks you can enjoy as a Knight. 


	Healthcare – We have a comprehensive medical coverage to ensure you, and your loved ones stay healthy.
	Elderly Care - At Wema Bank, our robust HMO plans include a provision for elderly care, allowing you to enroll one of your parents under your health coverage.
	Parental Leave (Paternity & Maternity) – Paid leave to support new parents, allowing them time to bond with their newborns.
	Year-End Bonus (13th Month) – As a Knight, there is an opportunity for you to enjoy an additional allowance as a reward for your dedication and hard work.
	Company Events – At Wema Bank, we engage in team-building activities, networking opportunities, and celebrations that foster a strong workplace culture.
	Competitive Pay – Attractive salary packages that reflect your skills, experience, and contributions.
	Leave Allowance – Financial support when you take your well-deserved time off.
	Profit Sharing – A share in the bank’s success, ensuring you, as an employee, benefit from the company’s growth.
    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

Inventory Control Officer Job at YangaBeauty




        
    

        
        At YangaBeauty, we believe in the transformative power of beauty. Founded by the visionary Jennifer Uloko, YangaBeauty is more than just a brand; it's a community. With a passion for excellence and a commitment to innovation, we bring you a range of beauty essentials designed to enhance your natural beauty and inspire self-love. Our range of high-quality products and services is designed to empower individuals, enhancing their natural beauty and boosting their confidence. With innovative formulas and vibrant shades that cater to diverse skin tones, Yanga Beauty is committed to providing the tools for every person to express their unique beauty.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Inventory Control Officer

Job Location: Lagos

Requirements


	Interested candidates should possess relevant qualifications with 2-3 years of experience.


Method of Application
